title: 谋杀启事
summary:
Chief Inspector Ridestad and Cora Crook discuss the possibility that the incident involving Rudi Scherz was a premeditated murder attempt rather than a prank. Cora proposes a theory that a foreign woman, possibly the "low-class" girl involved, manipulated the situation by entering through the kitchen to admit an assailant, shooting Scherz, and then staging a hysterical episode after locking herself in the dining room. While Ridestad notes that Edmund Sweeney confirmed the dining room door was locked from the inside, Cora points out that a back staircase door with a broken handle might allow access. Ridestad suggests checking the girl's papers and the revolver, noting the gun might not belong to Scherz. Cora argues that the motive for an attack on Miss Blacklock might be known by the girl and warns that someone might attempt the act again. Ridestad also advises keeping an eye on Miss Marple.

Later, Inspector Corradino visits Miss Blacklock to provide new information regarding Scherz’s criminal history. Corradino reveals that Scherz was not an innkeeper's son but a career criminal who had stolen jewelry in Bern, manipulated restaurant bills at a winter resort, and been involved in thefts at a Zurich department store. Scherz had fled Switzerland using forged documents and found work at the Royal Spa Hotel. Miss Blacklock questions why Scherz would target Chipping Clegg Horn if she possesses nothing of value. Miss Bonner suggests the newspaper advertisement was a warning of a planned murder attempt. Corradino notes that while the girl's documents appear to be in order, there may be someone else acting behind Scherz.
